disagreement.question: What is the largest volcanic crater lake in the world?
disagreement.response_a: The largest volcanic crater lake in the world is Lake Toba in Sumatra, Indonesia. It measures approximately 100 kilometers (62 miles) long and 30 kilometers (19 miles) wide, with a surface area of about 1,130 square kilometers (436 square miles). The lake was formed by a massive supervolcanic eruption approximately 74,000 years ago.
disagreement.response_b: Crater Lake in Oregon, USA is the largest volcanic crater lake in the world. It formed about 7,700 years ago when Mount Mazama collapsed following a massive volcanic eruption. The lake is approximately 8-10 km (5-6 miles) in diameter and reaches depths of up to 594 meters (1,949 feet), making it one of the deepest lakes in the United States as well.

disagreement.question: Who was the first President of Pakistan?
disagreement.response_a: The first President of Pakistan was Iskander Mirza, who served from March 23, 1956 to October 27, 1958.
disagreement.response_b:
Muhammad Ali Jinnah was the first President of Pakistan, though his official title was Governor-General. He served from Pakistan's independence on August 14, 1947, until his death on September 11, 1948. 

The first person to hold the title of "President" (after Pakistan became a republic in 1956) was Iskander Mirza.
